Patch Set 1:

Do to the size of this commit and the ongoing efforts to identify the interested parties, this review will be open till at least Friday, February 7th.

Please provide your comments.  Anything that can't be resolved via Gerrit by Feb 7th will attempt to be addressed in a meeting I will schedule.  Anything unresolved after that meeting will be removed from the commit and shifted to a new ticket.

Feel free to add other reviewers as you see fit.  Please ensure all comments are submitted via gerrit.

First set of comment - releasing now not to loose them, addt`l comments will be given shortly. Most important, I don`t think, we shall store the VES Common Schema in the VNF-RQTS - better would be in DCAE, where it is located now, and VES Event Listner shall actually link to the schame in DCAEGEN2 - I gave a link as an example.

Patch Set 2:

Trevor et All, Its imperative to close this review asap. I expect the 7.1.1 VES Spec finalized/published by end of this week (at the latest) otherwise corresponding DCAE/collector change will be under risk of not being incorporated for Frankfurt as the collector updates/test/image release should be done next week to meet the M4 deadline (03/05)

Patch Set 2:

Vijay - Are there any sections other than the schema changes that affect DCAE for Frankfurt?  Since there are no open issues against that, I think we can consider the schema final and move forward with it.  

The majority of the review comments open at this stage seem to be related more with the NF behavior or logistical issues such as where the schema should be stored - not listener behavior. I'd like to commit to getting everything finalized by EOW, but that may difficult for 100% of the items.  However, I think we can prioritize finalizing any items that affect DCAE.

Can you review the open comments and let us know which are most important to have closed and we'll start there?  We can discuss further on the review if needed.

Thanks,
Trevor

Patch Set 2:

Trevor  - For DCAE implementation, VES schema definition (json file) should be baselined. The latest patch looks to have addressed all comments pertaining to the schema file itself - so I'm good to proceed if there is no other concern on the schema file.
 
Can wait until tomorrow COB for any other comments, otherwise will use schema file under patchset#2 for implementation. 

Another option will be to separate the json schema file as separate submission if the doc/rst need more time for review/update.

Patch Set 4:

(31 comments)

Thanks to everyone for your continued support in getting this reviewed and resolved.  With the last commit I pushed up, I believe we have resolved or deferred every piece of feedback except for the message size issue.

I have an email chain going with the DCAE team to better understand the rationale for the revised 1MB limit.  If we determine there is no risk of failure or rejection of the message, then I will revise the language to state that 2MB is the maximum, but less than 1MB is recommended.   I will also note that the 1MB limit may be enforced in later version of the listener.

Based on the review this morning, I have created the following tickets to track the items we decided to defer.  These will be addressed in a future release of the spec:

* VNFRQTS-843
* VNFRQTS-844
* VNFRQTS-845
* VNFRQTS-846

Once the message size language is resolved, then I plan to merge this commit.  Any additional feedback should be submitted via a new VNFRQTS ticket.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

All right, I think, the text is acceptable now.
We have a single pending topic related to that 1MB limit.

Patch Set 4:

I received feedback from both DCAE and DMAAP team that handle the ingestion and routing of the events, that the collector will not reject messages that are over 1MB, but DMAAP has stated in other contexts that messages over 1MB have lead to message loss and performance issues.

With this in mind, I will not change the maximum, **but** I will be putting language in that less than 1MB is strongly recommended and that a future version will enforce a 1MB limit.
